post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 230
Newbie Game 91

On top of a steep, imposing mesa in the middle of the Naxera desert, under an endless, wide-open sky, there is a ring of stones. They are large and slate grey, irregular in shape and size. They spin slowly as they float, suspended a couple dozen feet in the air, glowing blue with lines of runic ore. During the day, it is almost a unremarkable sight, washed out by the harsh rays of the sun. But as dusk falls and the sky blooms, the desert glows in red and orange. Floating lights become visible, wisps of golden light that twinkle as they move, some bigger and brighter, others more dull. They gather at the top of the mesa, swirling around each other in a slow dance, before shooting up, up, up into the night sky to join the stars.

That is Sudzoof, the spout of the stars. And this is the story of how it tore a kingdom apart.

- Important Rules -
This tab summarizes the most important rules.

For experienced players: this is the only tab you need to read.
For new players: if you have time, familiarizing yourself with the rest of the rules can be helpful.

Integrity
  • Do not edit or delete your posts, even for typos. Just post again.
  • Do not OOT (Out of Thread) and talk about this game outside your role PM, QTs, approved Discord channels, or this thread, even if you are dead. Access to the spectator chats will be granted upon request.
  • Do not gamethrow or intentionally sabotage your wincondition.
  • Do not attach files or link to external documents concerning this game. Discussions must stay in this thread.
  • Do not use OGI (Out of Game Information). Publicly discussed reads should be made exclusively from information in this thread or past completed games.

Activity
  • Each "day" is 2 weeks while each "night" is 3 days.
  • Do not post during night.
  • This game uses Dawn start; Day 1 will not start until everyone shows that they have read the rules.
  • All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable.
  • Activity rules are 48 hours to in-game prod and 72 hours to PM prod.

Voting
  • You must format your votes correctly. Capitalization doesn't matter, and capital i/lowercase L are not differentiated.
  • Majority hang is off for Day 1, but it is on starting Day 2.
  • Deadline hang is on from Day 1 but it is off for LYLO.
  • Votes are locked at LYLO.

Etiquette and Warnings
  • Use common sense.
  • Do not use formatting that makes text hard to read.
  • You may attack a player’s opinion, but do not make personal attacks on players themselves.
  • First-timers can receive up to 3 warnings before their next offense results in a force replacement or modkill. All other players can receive up to 2.
  • All other rules are standard. If you break a rule that isn't covered here, you may still be warned.

Setup and Moderators
  • This game is based on the Matrix6 setup.
  • Mafia have access to daychat.
  • All role mechanics and Night Action Resolutions are standard.
  • This game is moderated by mathjay and Mathletesv.
  • This game operates optional discord spectator and mafia private chats using the Unpaid Intern Central server. PM the mods to request access.

Resources and Support
  • For any issues, you may contacts us through your Role PM or through Discord (ash in june#5081/Mathletesv#6308).
  • For a list of basic resources, please refer to the Resources tab below.
  • For an more extensive list of resources compiled by the UIMT, please go here.
  • The New Player Crisis Manual (Mk 1) is automatically PMed to all new players. A public version of the NPC Manual can be found here.
  • If this game is causing you significant stress or frustration, feel free to use your role PM to talk or vent. We may not be allowed to give you technical advice, but we can listen or help you find a better way to process events. You may also contact us via Discord if you prefer; you can ask us about this in your role PM.
  • Congratulations, you made it through the summary tab! If you've reached this point, go ahead and say poggers in the thread as a little souvenir.

  • General
    Credits to Binomial-theorem, chaotic_iak, willwang123, and fdas, among others, as well as the Mafia Universe forum rules, for the rule template.

    The following are standard mafia game rules.

    • Do not post during the night phase.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from the mods, including role PMs.
    • Do not talk about this game's events outside of this thread unless you are specifically given permission to do so by the moderators. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread. Referring to anything that has happened in an ongoing game, even if you do not say which game it is, is OOT.
    • Do not use any formatting that obscures or conceals text. This includes [color=#FFFFFF], [color=white], and [color=transparent].
    • Do not edit or delete posts, even for typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON.
    • If you are dead, do not post, and do not talk about this game anywhere else. You may still spectate, and access to the spoiled and unspoiled spectator chats will be offered.
    • Do not impersonate the mods.
    • Do not lie to the mods.
    • Do not abuse loopholes in the rules. Intent will be considered during rule enforcement.
    • You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications.

  • Votes and Hangs
    This tab covers voting, unvoting, No Hangs, and hangs.

    • Votes must be in this format: Vote: [player username] to be counted.
    • Usernames in votes must be spelled, but not necessarily capitalized, correctly. Do not use nicknames.
    • For the purposes of voting, I (capital i) and l (lowercase L) are considered identical.
    • For example, both Vote: mathjay and Vote: mAtHjAy are valid votes, but vote: mathjay, Vote: jay, Vote: mathjay., Vote:mathjay, Vote: mathjay, and Vote: mathjay are invalid.
    • Votes followed by extraneous punctuation that is bolded, even with separate bold tags, are invalid.
    • Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags do not count.
    • Vote to hang no one by posting Vote: No Hang.
    • Unvote by posting Unvote. You do not have to unvote before voting again.
    • Invalid votes will not count under any circumstances. Please consider notifying the mods in your role PM if you want to purposely fakevote.
    • Valid votes will always be counted unless they are clearly unintentional. There is no guarantee that an accidental vote will not be counted.
    • This game uses deadline hang: the player with the most votes at the EOD (End of Day) deadline will be hanged.
    • If two or more players have the same number of votes when the deadline is reached, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Starting Day 2, if a player is voted by the majority of living players, then that player will be hanged immediately; and if No Hang is voted by half of living players, a No Hang will occur.
    • Upon reaching LYLO (Hang-or-Lose), votes will be locked, meaning that they cannot be changed.
    • Deadline hang is off at LYLO. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, resulting in an automatic win for the mafia team.
    • The moment the deadline occurs, no further votes will be counted. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • If the deadline has passed or a hammer has occurred and the EOD has not been declared, the game will proceed into twilight. Players may still post, but no more votes or unvotes will be counted.
    • Once the moderators declare the EOD, the person hanged will be officially eliminated and their role revealed. Night will then immediately begin.
  • Activity
    This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.

    • All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
    • Each day phase will be 336 hours (14 days), and each night phase will be 72 hours (3 days).
    • The moderators may extend deadlines at their discretion.
    • If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform a NA that night.
    • Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
    • Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night. You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates in the case of a missed deadline.
    • Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence) by posting the V/LA dates in bold in-thread or in their role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded.
    • V/LAs should be shorter than one week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for more options.
    • Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request replacement by posting Request Replacement in-thread or in their role PM.
    • Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in.
    • Players will receive an in-game prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Players will receive a PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA.
    • Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 108 hours and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement.
    • If a player receives 4 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
    • If a player reaches replacement status more than once, they will be replaced.
    • If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. The moderators will send a notification via PM to the specified player, but this will not count as an official prod.
    • If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
  • Integrity and Etiquette
    This tab covers integrity recommendations, player etiquette, warning limits, and examples of inconsiderate gameplay.

    • Do not gamethrow.
    • Do not discuss player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, and other interactions with moderators.
    • Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive inter-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.
    • Do not publicly reference OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or create situations outside the thread that players could reasonably think as alignment indicative.

    The above rules are attempts to preserve the spirit of the game and should not affect most players. For specific details and examples, please click here.

    • Do not make personal attacks.
    • Do not spam.
    • Behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning.
    • Follow AoPS Community Guidelines.
    • Be mature and use common sense.
    • For minor rule infractions, first-timers can receive up to three warnings, while all other players can receive up to two. It is possible to receive multiple warnings for a single infraction.

    The above rules broadly cover most etiquette issues, but for specific details and examples of inconsiderate gameplay, please click here.

  • Moderation Policies
    This tab has been redacted due to a lack of space in the OP. Please refer to other versions of OCRSTM 8.

  • Moderator Information
    This tab provides technical information about this game.

    • mathjay and Mathletesv are the moderators of Mafia Game 230.
    • Other Unpaid Interns may contribute to flavor, OCRSTM, feedback, and the management of the private chat discord system.
    • This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be granted access to the server as appropriate.
    • Red text is used for votecounts, warnings, and other announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related text.
    • Blue text is used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators.
    • Green text is used for town flavor-related text.
    • Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes.
    • Current custom shades are: Town flavor green (#00f752), Activity Check blue (#005dff), mathjay's blue (#4a86e8), HannahW's blue (#0b5394), Mathletesv's blue (#4d94ff), Scipio1's blue (#3d70ff), Socoobo's orange (#ff8000).
    • The Not Voting section should be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
    • If an error in the votecount is not pointed out within 2 days or by the next EOD, whichever is sooner, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • The moderators may change the rules at any time. Major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OCRSTM Version History.
    • Players will not be retroactively penalized for violating new rules.
    • Major changes to the OP may be made in between games. The most important changes will be noted in OCRSTM Version History.
    • The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make the final judgement in the moderation of this game.

    MG230 OP is based on Overcomplicated RulesetTM (OCRSTM) version 8.
    Current version: 8.3
    Version History
Setup
This game is based on the Matrix6 setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Before the game starts, the setup will be randomly chosen from 6 possible role combinations:
  • Setup 1: 1 Town Jailkeeper, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
  • Setup 2: 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
  • Setup 3: 1 Town Tracker, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
  • Setup 4/A: 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
  • Setup 5/B: 1 Town Cop, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
  • Setup 6/C: 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
There are a couple other setup mechanics that should be clarified:
  • This game is Dawn start. Once all the players confirm, Dawn will end, Day 1 will start, and the EOD deadline will be 14 days from then.
  • Doctors cannot protect themselves two nights in a row.
  • A Mafia Roleblocker's action takes precedence over a Town Jailkeeper's action.
  • A Mafia Roleblocker can kill and roleblock on the same night.
  • Cop results are sane.
  • Mafia have daychat.
Role PMs
Secretary de l’Rexalis (Vanilla Townie) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are a Secretary de l’Rexalis (Vanilla Townie). You’re one of the prince’s most trusted advisors. You and your colleagues keep the kingdom running smoothly while the prince is still learning the ropes. Or forgets things. Or doesn’t want to get out of bed.

When the castle was attacked, you and eight other secretaries evacuated into the safe room, built deep in the depths of the castle. Designed to survive a full-scale siege for weeks and function as a temporary command center, the room was a safe place to analyze the situation and come up with a plan. Then a messenger scroll from the prince appeared. It had been sent in a hurry; the prince’s usually elegant scrawl was messy, and the message itself was very short. ”Two of you are traitors,” it read, with no preamble whatsoever. ”Can’t help. Danger. Care.”

The room is completely silent. Experimentally, you try to open the heavy stone doors, but you already know that it won’t budge. You’re trapped here, in the middle of a war, and you don’t know when help will arrive. How do you survive?

Your power lies in your observation skills, your voice, and your vote.

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

You yourself don’t have much affinity for magic, but one of your past partners gifted you with a phoenix talisman. It’s a small emerald pendant that you wear around your neck all the time; you don’t know whether they made it, or how they found something so rare. But you’re keeping it safe, and thankfully enough, it hasn’t had to activate itself so far.

Secretary d’Exalis (Mafia Goon) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are a Secretary d’Exalis (Mafia Goon). For over twenty years, you served the royal family faithfully. You started as a clerk, working your way up the ladder before you were granted the position of a royal secretary. You watched as the crown prince grew into a capable young man. And when you heard that the prince was planning to destroy Sudzoof, you hoped it wasn’t true.

The night before the demolition ceremony, you stood outside in the shadow of a tree, looking for your mother’s stars. It had been a pastime of hers, like knitting and weaving, and she had hung a good number of stars in the sky. But ever since she had passed, two weeks ago, they’d been getting fainter, until he could hardly find them. Soon, the only thing left would be her name, engraved on the stones of Sudzoof. You made a decision then.

When you saw the explosion in your magic mirror, watching silently from a deserted hallway in the castle, you just felt cold. The mirror played no sound as the great floating ring of boulders fell from the sky, crashing into the mesa and plummeting towards the desert below. When cloaked mages started attacking the castle, you tucked your mirror away, joining the other secretaries as they entered the safe room and locked the door. You listened with half your attention as they received the crown prince’s warning letter. You watched, lost in your head, as they tried to unlock the door.

Ba-bump, ba-bump, ba-bump, insisted your heartbeat, hot and loud and so real it hurt. Your hands were shaking, so you took a deep breath and gathered your composure. Then you went to discuss what to do with other secretaries.

Your partner is [player] ([role]), and you may discuss with them in this QuickTopic while you are alive. One of you may submit a factional kill each night to eliminate a player.

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal is to stay hidden and kill Town. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

Secretary de l’Arkhelion (Mafia Roleblocker) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are the Secretary de l’Arkhelion (Mafia Roleblocker). As the guardian of the Royal Libraries and an official curator of the archives, you have spent your life cataloging the things people leave behind. Sudzoof is... was one of the few things you genuinely admired in this world. Not only was the craftsmanship of the runic enchantments exquisitely intricate, but whoever designed it to automatically keep records was a truly enlightened individual.

And then the crown prince had it destroyed as soon as he ascended the throne. “Who cares if they’re gone?” you had heard more than a few people say. “No one reads them.” You found himself unable to disagree with the latter point, yet you still couldn’t suppress an instinctive hurl of revulsion at the thought of what the prince had done. A hundred years of history and thousands of names gone, just like that. Disgusting.

Maybe that’s why you made the impulsive choice, even though the consequences might be steep. In the end, you are a Nullmage, one of the few who still know the art. A valuable asset to either side, which is why you can afford to sit on the line until a clearer path emerges. After all, you’d already bargained for the safety of your archives, and was that not the most important thing?

Every night, you may PM the mods the name of a player. You will then roleblock that person for that night, preventing their night actions, if any, from resolving. Your action takes precedence over the Jailkeeper, if applicable.

Your partner is [player] ([role]), and you may discuss with them in this QuickTopic while you are alive. One of you may submit a factional kill each night to eliminate a player. You can simultaneously perform both the factional kill and your roleblock.

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal is to stay hidden and kill Town. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

[center][size=175]Welcome to Mafia Game 230[/size]
[size=125]Newbie Game 91

[img]https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/443067503661285377/830070049003405312/image0.png[/img][/center]

On top of a steep, imposing mesa in the middle of the Naxera desert, under an endless, wide-open sky, there is a ring of stones. They are large and slate grey, irregular in shape and size. They spin slowly as they float, suspended a couple dozen feet in the air, glowing blue with lines of runic ore. During the day, it is almost a unremarkable sight, washed out by the harsh rays of the sun. But as dusk falls and the sky blooms, the desert glows in red and orange. Floating lights become visible, wisps of golden light that twinkle as they move, some bigger and brighter, others more dull. They gather at the top of the mesa, swirling around each other in a slow dance, before shooting up, up, up into the night sky to join the stars. 

That is Sudzoof, the spout of the stars. And this is the story of how it tore a kingdom apart. 

[hide=- Important Rules -]
[b]This tab summarizes the most important rules.[/b]

[b]For experienced players:[/b] this is the only tab you need to read. 
[b]For new players:[/b] if you have time, familiarizing yourself with the rest of the rules can be helpful. 

[b]Integrity[/b]
[list][*][b]Do not edit or delete[/b] your posts, even for typos. Just post again. 
[*][b]Do not OOT (Out of Thread)[/b] and talk about this game outside your role PM, QTs, approved Discord channels, or this thread, even if you are dead. Access to the spectator chats will be granted upon request. 
[*][b]Do not gamethrow[/b] or intentionally sabotage your wincondition. 
[*][b]Do not attach files[/b] or link to external documents concerning this game. Discussions must stay in this thread.
[*][b]Do not use OGI[/b] (Out of Game Information). Publicly discussed reads should be made exclusively from information in this thread or past completed games. [/list]

[b]Activity[/b]
[list][*]Each [b]"day" is 2 weeks[/b] while each [b]"night" is 3 days[/b].
[*][b]Do not post during night.[/b]
[*]This game uses [b]Dawn start;[/b] Day 1 will not start until everyone shows that they have read the rules. 
[*]All [b]times[/b] will be in [b]Eastern Standard Time[/b] (EST, UTC-5) or [b]Eastern Daylight Time[/b] (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable. 
[*]Activity rules are [b]48 hours to in-game prod[/b] and [b]72 hours to PM prod.[/b][/list]

[b]Voting[/b]
[list][*]You [b]must format your votes correctly.[/b] Capitalization doesn't matter, and capital i/lowercase L are not differentiated. 
[*][b]Majority hang is off[/b] for Day 1, but it is [b]on[/b] starting Day 2.
[*][b]Deadline hang is on[/b] from Day 1 but it is [b]off[/b] for LYLO. 
[*]Votes are [b]locked at LYLO.[/b][/list]

[b]Etiquette and Warnings[/b]

[list][*][b]Use common sense.[/b] 
[*]Do not use formatting that makes text [b]hard to read.[/b]
[*]You may attack a player’s opinion, but [b]do not make personal attacks[/b] on players themselves. 
[*]First-timers can receive up to [b]3 warnings[/b] before their next offense results in a force replacement or modkill. [b]All other players can receive up to 2. [/b]
[*][b]All other rules are standard.[/b] If you break a rule that isn't covered here, you may still be warned.[/list] 

[b]Setup and Moderators[/b]

[list][*]This game is based on the [b]Matrix6 setup. [/b]
[*]Mafia have access to [b]daychat[/b]. 
[*]All role mechanics and Night Action Resolutions are [b]standard. [/b]
[*]This game is moderated by mathjay and Mathletesv. 
[*]This game operates optional discord spectator and mafia private chats using the Unpaid Intern Central server. PM the mods to request access.[/list]

[b]Resources and Support[/b]

[list][*]For any issues, you may contacts us through your Role PM or through Discord (ash in june#5081/Mathletesv#6308). 
[*]For a list of basic resources, please refer to the Resources tab below. 
[*]For an more extensive list of resources compiled by the UIMT, please go [url=https://artofproblemsolving.com/community/c1980342h2517106_important_resources]here[/url].
[*]The New Player Crisis Manual (Mk 1) is automatically PMed to all new players. A public version of the NPC Manual can be found [url=https://artofproblemsolving.com/community/c1980342h2517631_new_player_crisis_manual_8]here[/url].
[*]If this game is causing you significant stress or frustration, feel free to use your role PM to talk or vent. We may not be allowed to give you technical advice, but we can listen or help you find a better way to process events. You may also contact us via Discord if you prefer; you can ask us about this in your role PM. 
[*]Congratulations, you made it through the summary tab! If you've reached this point, go ahead and say poggers in the thread as a little souvenir. [/list][/hide]

[list][*][hide=General]
[i]Credits to [url=http://bit.ly/fbith]Binomial-theorem[/url], [url=http://bit.ly/rmchao]chaotic_iak[/url], [url=http://bit.ly/rmww123]willwang123[/url], and [url=http://bit.ly/ffdas]fdas[/url], among others, as well as the [url=http://bit.ly/MUnFR]Mafia Universe forum rules[/url], for the rule template.[/i]

[b]The following are standard mafia game rules.[/b]


[list][*]Do not post during the night phase.
[*]Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from the mods, including role PMs.
[*]Do not talk about this game's events outside of this thread unless you are specifically given permission to do so by the moderators. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread. Referring to anything that has happened in an ongoing game, even if you do not say which game it is, is OOT.
[*]Do not use any formatting that obscures or conceals text. This includes [verbatim][color=#FFFFFF], [color=white], and [color=transparent][/verbatim]. 
[*]Do not edit or delete posts, even for typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON. 
[*]If you are dead, do not post, and do not talk about this game anywhere else. You may still spectate, and access to the spoiled and unspoiled spectator chats will be offered. 
[*]Do not impersonate the mods. 
[*]Do not lie to the mods. 
[*]Do not abuse loopholes in the rules. Intent will be considered during rule enforcement. 
[*]You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications.[/list][/hide]

[*][hide=Votes and Hangs]
[b]This tab covers voting, unvoting, No Hangs, and hangs.[/b]


[list][*]Votes must be in this format: [b]Vote: [player username][/b] to be counted.
[*]Usernames in votes must be spelled, but not necessarily capitalized, correctly. Do not use nicknames. 
[*]For the purposes of voting, I (capital i) and l (lowercase L) are considered identical. 
[*]For example, both [b]Vote: mathjay[/b] and [b]Vote: mAtHjAy[/b] are valid votes, but [b]vote: mathjay[/b], [b]Vote: jay[/b], [b]Vote: mathjay.[/b], [b]Vote:mathjay[/b], Vote: [b]mathjay[/b], and [b][i]Vote: mathjay[/i][/b] are invalid. 
[*]Votes followed by extraneous punctuation that is bolded, even with separate bold tags, are invalid. 
[*]Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags do not count.
[*]Vote to hang no one by posting [b]Vote: No Hang[/b].
[*]Unvote by posting [b]Unvote[/b]. You do not have to unvote before voting again. 
[*]Invalid votes will not count under any circumstances. Please consider notifying the mods in your role PM if you want to purposely fakevote.
[*]Valid votes will always be counted unless they are clearly unintentional. There is no guarantee that an accidental vote will not be counted.
[*]This game uses deadline hang: the player with the most votes at the EOD (End of Day) deadline will be hanged. 
[*]If two or more players have the same number of votes when the deadline is reached, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]Starting Day 2, if a player is voted by the majority of living players, then that player will be hanged immediately; and if No Hang is voted by half of living players, a No Hang will occur.
[*]Upon reaching LYLO (Hang-or-Lose), votes will be locked, meaning that they cannot be changed.
[*]Deadline hang is off at LYLO. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, resulting in an automatic win for the mafia team. 
[*]The moment the deadline occurs, no further votes will be counted. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]If the deadline has passed or a hammer has occurred and the EOD has not been declared, the game will proceed into twilight. Players may still post, but no more votes or unvotes will be counted.
[*]Once the moderators declare the EOD, the person hanged will be officially eliminated and their role revealed. Night will then immediately begin.[/list][/hide]
[*][hide=Activity]
[b]This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.[/b]


[list][*]All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
[*]Each day phase will be 336 hours (14 days), and each night phase will be 72 hours (3 days). 
[*]The moderators may extend deadlines at their discretion.
[*]If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform a NA that night.
[*]Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
[*]Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night. You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates in the case of a missed deadline. 
[*]Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence) by posting the V/LA dates in [b]bold[/b] in-thread or in their role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded. 
[*]V/LAs should be shorter than one week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for more options. 
[*]Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request replacement by posting [b]Request Replacement[/b] in-thread or in their role PM. 
[*]Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in. 
[*]Players will receive an in-game prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
[*]Players will receive a PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA. 
[*]Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 108 hours and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement. 
[*]If a player receives 4 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
[*]If a player reaches replacement status more than once, they will be replaced.
[*]If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. The moderators will send a notification via PM to the specified player, but this will not count as an official prod.
[*]If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.[list][/hide]
[*][hide=Integrity and Etiquette]
[b]This tab covers integrity recommendations, player etiquette, warning limits, and examples of inconsiderate gameplay.[/b]


[list][*]Do not gamethrow. 
[*]Do not discuss player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, and other interactions with moderators. 
[*]Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive inter-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.
[*]Do not publicly reference OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or create situations outside the thread that players could reasonably think as alignment indicative. [/list]

The above rules are attempts to preserve the spirit of the game and should not affect most players. For specific details and examples, [url=https://aops.com/community/p21335214]please click here.[/url]


[list][*]Do not make personal attacks.
[*]Do not spam. 
[*]Behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning. 
[*]Follow AoPS Community Guidelines. 
[*]Be mature and use common sense. 
[*]For minor rule infractions, first-timers can receive up to three warnings, while all other players can receive up to two. It is possible to receive multiple warnings for a single infraction.[/list]

The above rules broadly cover most etiquette issues, but for specific details and examples of inconsiderate gameplay, [url=https://aops.com/community/p21335246]please click here.[/url][/hide]

[*][hide=Moderation Policies]
[b]This tab has been redacted due to a lack of space in the OP. Please refer to other versions of OCRSTM 8. [/b]
[/hide]

[*][hide=Moderator Information]
[b]This tab provides technical information about this game.[/b]


[list][*]mathjay and Mathletesv are the moderators of Mafia Game 230. 
[*]Other Unpaid Interns may contribute to flavor, OCRSTM, feedback, and the management of the private chat discord system. 
[*]This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be granted access to the server as appropriate. 
[*][color=#FF0000]Red text[/color] is used for votecounts, warnings, and other announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related text.
[*][color=#0000FF]Blue text[/color] is used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators. 
[*][color=#00FF00]Green text[/color] is used for town flavor-related text.
[*]Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes. 
[*]Current custom shades are: Town flavor green ([color=#00f752]#00f752[/color]), Activity Check blue ([color=#005dff]#005dff[/color]), mathjay's blue ([color=#4a86e8]#4a86e8[/color]), HannahW's blue ([color=#0b5394]#0b5394[/color]), Mathletesv's blue ([color=#4d94ff]#4d94ff[/color]), Scipio1's blue ([color=#3d70ff]#3d70ff[/color]), Socoobo's orange ([color=#ff8000]#ff8000[/color]).
[*]The Not Voting section should be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
[*]If an error in the votecount is not pointed out within 2 days or by the next EOD, whichever is sooner, the incorrect votecount will become official.
[*]The moderators may change the rules at any time. Major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OCRSTM Version History. 
[*]Players will not be retroactively penalized for violating new rules. 
[*]Major changes to the OP may be made in between games. The most important changes will be noted in OCRSTM Version History. 
[*]The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make the final judgement in the moderation of this game. [/list]

[b]MG230 OP is based on Overcomplicated Ruleset[sup]TM[/sup] (OCRSTM) version 8.[/b]
[i]Current version: 8.3[/i]
[url=https://aops.com/community/p21335350]Version History[/url]
[/hide][/list]
[hide=Setup]This game is based on the [url=http://bit.ly/m6msw]Matrix6[/url] setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Before the game starts, the setup will be randomly chosen from 6 possible role combinations:
[list][*][b]Setup 1:[/b] 1 Town Jailkeeper, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
[*][b]Setup 2:[/b] 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
[*][b]Setup 3:[/b] 1 Town Tracker, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
[*][b]Setup 4/A:[/b] 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
[*][b]Setup 5/B:[/b] 1 Town Cop, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
[*][b]Setup 6/C:[/b] 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons[/list]
There are a couple other setup mechanics that should be clarified: 
[list][*]This game is Dawn start. Once all the players confirm, Dawn will end, Day 1 will start, and the EOD deadline will be 14 days from then. 
[*]Doctors cannot protect themselves two nights in a row.
[*]A Mafia Roleblocker's action takes precedence over a Town Jailkeeper's action.
[*]A Mafia Roleblocker can kill and roleblock on the same night.
[*]Cop results are sane.
[*]Mafia have daychat.[/list]
[hide=Role PMs]
[quote="Secretary de l’Rexalis (Vanilla Townie)"]
Hello, [player]!

You are a [color=#00f752]Secretary de l’Rexalis (Vanilla Townie)[/color]. You’re one of the prince’s most trusted advisors. You and your colleagues keep the kingdom running smoothly while the prince is still learning the ropes. Or forgets things. Or doesn’t want to get out of bed. 

When the castle was attacked, you and eight other secretaries evacuated into the safe room, built deep in the depths of the castle. Designed to survive a full-scale siege for weeks and function as a temporary command center, the room was a safe place to analyze the situation and come up with a plan. Then a messenger scroll from the prince appeared. It had been sent in a hurry; the prince’s usually elegant scrawl was messy, and the message itself was very short. [i]”Two of you are traitors,”[/i] it read, with no preamble whatsoever. [i]”Can’t help. Danger. Care.”[/i]

The room is completely silent. Experimentally, you try to open the heavy stone doors, but you already know that it won’t budge. You’re trapped here, in the middle of a war, and you don’t know when help will arrive. How do you survive? 

Your power lies in your observation skills, your voice, and your vote. 

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

Hello, [player]!

You are the [color=#00f752]Secretary de l’Cikiel (1-shot Bulletproof Townie)[/color]. A former officer in the Vaeryal General Army, you are in charge of the castle security. You yourself don’t have much affinity for magic, but one of your past partners gifted you with a phoenix talisman. It’s a small emerald pendant that you wear around your neck all the time; you don’t know whether they made it, or how they found something so rare. But you’re keeping it safe, and thankfully enough, it hasn’t had to activate itself so far.

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

[quote="Secretary d’Exalis (Mafia Goon)"]
Hello, [player]!

You are a [color=red]Secretary d’Exalis (Mafia Goon)[/color]. For over twenty years, you served the royal family faithfully. You started as a clerk, working your way up the ladder before you were granted the position of a royal secretary. You watched as the crown prince grew into a capable young man. And when you heard that the prince was planning to destroy Sudzoof, you hoped it wasn’t true. 

The night before the demolition ceremony, you stood outside in the shadow of a tree, looking for your mother’s stars. It had been a pastime of hers, like knitting and weaving, and she had hung a good number of stars in the sky. But ever since she had passed, two weeks ago, they’d been getting fainter, until he could hardly find them. Soon, the only thing left would be her name, engraved on the stones of Sudzoof. You made a decision then. 

When you saw the explosion in your magic mirror, watching silently from a deserted hallway in the castle, you just felt cold. The mirror played no sound as the great floating ring of boulders fell from the sky, crashing into the mesa and plummeting towards the desert below. When cloaked mages started attacking the castle, you tucked your mirror away, joining the other secretaries as they entered the safe room and locked the door. You listened with half your attention as they received the crown prince’s warning letter. You watched, lost in your head, as they tried to unlock the door. 

[i]Ba-bump, ba-bump, ba-bump,[/i] insisted your heartbeat, hot and loud and so real it hurt. Your hands were shaking, so you took a deep breath and gathered your composure. Then you went to discuss what to do with other secretaries. 

Your partner is [player] ([role]), and you may discuss with them in [url=http://bit.ly/MafQT]this QuickTopic[/url] while you are alive. One of you may submit a factional kill each night to eliminate a player. 

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal is to stay hidden and kill Town. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

[quote="Secretary de l’Arkhelion (Mafia Roleblocker)"]
Hello, [player]!

You are the [color=red]Secretary de l’Arkhelion (Mafia Roleblocker)[/color]. As the guardian of the Royal Libraries and an official curator of the archives, you have spent your life cataloging the things people leave behind. Sudzoof is... was one of the few things you genuinely admired in this world. Not only was the craftsmanship of the runic enchantments exquisitely intricate, but whoever designed it to automatically keep records was a truly enlightened individual. 

And then the crown prince had it destroyed as soon as he ascended the throne. “Who cares if they’re gone?” you had heard more than a few people say. “No one reads them.” You found himself unable to disagree with the latter point, yet you still couldn’t suppress an instinctive hurl of revulsion at the thought of what the prince had done. A hundred years of history and thousands of names gone, just like that. Disgusting. 

Maybe that’s why you made the impulsive choice, even though the consequences might be steep. In the end, you are a Nullmage, one of the few who still know the art. A valuable asset to either side, which is why you can afford to sit on the line until a clearer path emerges. After all, you’d already bargained for the safety of your archives, and was that not the most important thing?

post_rendered:
Three weeks ago, Palace d’Aleum.


“Hey,” said Mathletesv.

mathjay turned to look at him. They were in the prince’s private lounge, which was scattered with couches. Mathletesv was nestled into a dark blue armchair, while he had draped himself over a particularly plump couch covered in velvet. He was idly spinning stars out of the residue magic leaching from the wards cast on the palace; it didn’t take much concentration to gather the loose threads of magic in the air and twirl it into a loose orb, before sending it on its way to Sudzoof.

“Remember when I said back then? That I’d get rid of Sudzoof.” Mathletesv looked unusually serious as he said that, with his hand under his hand and with a little frown, like he was trying to figure out what to say.

mathjay stilled, not really sure where the conversation was going. The small bundle of energy floating in front of his finger began to unravel, barely visible in the afternoon sunlight as it dissipated back into equilibrium.

“I’m going to inherit the crown in just three more weeks,” Mathletesv continued. His eyes were firmly fixed somewhere on the wall.

“You are,” mathjay agreed quietly. He stared at the prince, responses ranging from “You know I know this,” to “Congratulations?” hanging on the tip of his tongue. But he decided to wait.

His patience ran out 15 seconds later. The prince was still staring resolutely at the wall like it held the answer to all of his problems, and the only sound in the room was the crackling of the fireplace. So he asked.

“Are you going to do it?”

Mathletesv finally looked at him. “I don’t know,” he said simply.

* * * * *

Current time, Naxera Desert.


“...did more harm than good. It became an obsession for some people. It has caused rivalries to be tainted bitter, friends to turn into enemies, and sparked exhausting fight over exhausting fight. The beautiful stars which once united us, Sudzoof turned them into catalysts of discord. Many years ago, I swore to destroy it. And today, I finally will.”

“Of course, I know what our stars mean to us. None of that will change. We will still celebrate unions by creating new constellations, and hang shimmering, radiant pole stars to grant our loved ones guidance and good luck. We will still weave tapestries of light in the night sky and celebrate the Festival de l’Asteri. Retiring Sudzoof does not change any of this. We will simply go back to the way things were, to a better time.”

“After one hundred years... I am sure we can all see that the right choice here is to say farewell,” Mathletesv said. He picked up the stone tablet in front of him. Engraved on it was a trigger rune, almost completed; drawing a bead of liquid flame from his finger, he finished it. Almost immediately, the tablet glows white hot for a moment before fading away, leaving a slab of charred, crumbling stone behind. “Goodbye,” he said, and turned towards his left.

There, in the distance, stood the tall, imposing mountain of rock. At the very top, there was a flash of light and smoke. Slowly, the giant, floating boulders of Sudzoof began to fall, faster and faster until they hit the top of the mesa, kicking up huge clouds of reddish-orange dirt. That was when the rumbling thunder of the explosion finally hit, rolling across the desert like a giant wave.

Boom.

Mathletesv demolishes Sudzoof. He was the Crown Prince of Vaeryal (Moderator 2).

As he looked across the crowd as they watched the mesa collapse, he suddenly noticed mathjay, standing at the edge of the crowd. He was staring directly at him, gaze intense and unblinking, and it unsettled him enough that it took another moment for him to realize that mathjay’s eyes were glowing gold.

”When I try really, really hard to make something disappear, my eyes get really bright and scary! It’s the funnest thing ever if you do it on Halloween and chase after people! C’mon, I’ll show you!”

Eyes widening, he was just about to open his mouth to sound the alarm when, with the sound of a thousand windows shattering at once, the illusion broke. The world shifted slightly, and hundreds of hooded figures shimmered into existence. Some of them held weapons, others wielded their respective elements, and after one, horrible moment of motionlessness, they ripped into action.

They cut into the crowd, hacking and slicing as the clearing descended into chaos. Pillars of earth and vortexes of air ravaged the area as the royal guards desperately tried to get people to safety. Mathletesv stood still, in shock as he tried to process what was happening.

“Your Highness! We need to go, now!”

He vaguely felt himself nodding as his guards took him by the arms. Then he suddenly got hit by vertigo as his perspective... shifted until he was watching his guards ushering him... away. Into a waiting air carriage.

Confused, he looked up to see mathjay standing in front of him, grinning ferally while engraving a cage of runes around him with light. His feet were already bound and he could tell that he wouldn’t be able to move in a couple more seconds.

“What are you doing?” he asked him.

mathjay didn’t answer.

Mathletesv shook his head to try to clear it. “Even if you kidnap me, you’re not going to win. Not the long game, not with those numbers. The Secretaries are still alive, and they’ve been running the kingdom for as long as I can remember. They’re going to organize a counterattack, and you won’t survive it.”

mathjay looked amused. “The castle should be overrun by now. I suppose that your secretaries will be retreating to the safe room, but... allow me to ask you this.” He paused, before tilting his head to the side like a dog. “What if I told you that the loyalties of two of them weren’t quite what you thought they were?” mathjay said, gleeful malice in his voice.

“I...” Mathletesv’s head was spinning and he could barely think straight, but he managed to summon a messenger scroll and a pen. “Why is...” He couldn’t tell if he was writing the words properly. “I...”

With a sneer, mathjay finished the rune with a slash of his hand just as Mathletesv sent the scroll. There was a flash of blinding light.

mathjay betrays his kingdom. He was the Lumimage of 94,664 stars (Moderator 1).

post_rendered:
Ok. Information Post number one:

1. Try to limit fluff (posts that do not contribute to the game). This means not following the examples of Scipio and Bob just above.

2. But try to be active and give thoughts with explanations.
For example, I don't particularly like how Scipio straight up claimed mafia. One could conjecture that mafia would not do this since they want to stay hidden, but Scipio is an experienced player and thus knows that him claiming does not out his alignment in any conclusive way. There have also been multiple games that open with people trolling mafia claims (in fact most nonnewbie games). Ultimately, what Scipio did doesn't actually tell me anything about his alignment. It just is an unproductive start that served as an example for giving thoughts.
^ The above thought isn't particularly good since there's not much so far, but as your proceed, expect more thorough and conclusive thoughts.

3. Ask questions if you have them. You can also ask people questions to probe for information regarding their alignments.

post_rendered:
Read this:
HannahW wrote:
Because snoozing's plagiarized resource is quite difficult to read, let me revise it:

Goal: find scum
  1. Be active and post your thoughts (but do not fluff and clog up the thread).
  2. If you're confused, ask questions. Even if you're not confused, ask questions. Question why people did this or that. How did they reach a conclusion. Question people you suspect. Why did they did not do something and so on.
  3. And then consider the answers. Would scum or town be more likely to do something? Would scum or town be more likely to answer this way. Would scum want to do something more than town? Would town want to do something more than scum? And why.
  4. Try to be able to explain why you think this way and explain your reads.
terms
General:
  • RQS - Random questioning stage.
  • Reads - thoughts on who is scum and who is town.
  • meta - how one has played in mafia games.
  • wifom - wine in front of me
  • AI - Alignment indicative. Means that the said thing tells you whether the person is scum or town.
  • NAI - Not alignment indicative.
  • v/v - villager/villager. Means that both players mentioned are town.
  • w/w - wolf/wolf. Means that both players mentioned are scum [together].
  • w/v or v/w - One scum, one wolf.
Textbook scumtells:
  • bandwagon - to follow the crowd.
  • buddying - when a scum townreads another scum, perhaps without a good reason, to try to save their partner or make them tr'ed.
  • bussing - when a scum attacks another scum to make it look like they cannot be partners.
  • hedge - to have no definitive stance, to fencesit.
  • lurk - to fail to show up deliberately.
  • pocketing - scum sweet talking or otherwise coaxing a town into townreading them.

Also, adding to what Bob said, don't just not claim PR. Also don't claim Vanilla Townie either. Here is why:
HannahW wrote:
Hmm. One other thing that is always missed for some reason:
Do not claim if you are town, whether you are a PR (power role - you have a special power as town) or a VT (vanilla townie... normal person with only a vote). I repeat. Do not claim whether you are a PR or a VT if you are town unless you're about to die, which won't happen for another 12 days.
The rationale behind this is so that you don't help scum narrow down who is or isn't a PR so that they can kill or block those who are a PR. So don't specify whether you are or are not a PR/VT.

lmk if you are not going to read all that. But I highly recommend reading it. It's better than having to repeat the same thing to you.

post_rendered:
so one thing town commonly uses to find scum is POE, which stands for process of elimination

so pretty much, instead of finding scum, you find town.

the people who you are very sure are town go into the towncore, which pretty much means that they won't ever be hanged

from there, you hang the scummiest of the players not in the towncore, then the 2nd scummiest, etc.

if you have a big enough POE (that's also accurate), then town pretty much autowins, they don't have to scumhunt, they just hang everyone not in the towncore

Ok, so here's my problem @sparkid.

So, yes, there's nothing wrong with trying to get magic's opinion, and my problems with it is just personal (I think it's unnecessary which I can elaborate on if you want) so like go ahead if you want ig.

However, saying that you don't trust the SEs because we're competent is frankly just stupid. Yes, we're competent. Yes, we all have good scumgames. Does that mean you shouldn't trust us? Not exactly. I'm not saying you should blindly trust us, because that's also just stupid, but it doesn't mean our opinions are "untrustworthy". So in normal games, most people are competent/experienced (most because some newbies finish their newbie game without learning much) and nobody goes like that. We don't blindly trust people, but we don't not listen to people either. We're supposed to share ideas, reads, things we noticed, so that other people can comment and discuss them so people can keep working and improving on their reads.

So what I'm saying is you shouldn't be like "your reads are untrustworthy". You can disagree, point out things wrong with them, even scumread us for them is there's valid reasoning, but ignoring them because we're competent? That's not what should be happening.

Hope that helped.

So what I'm saying is you shouldn't be like "your reads are untrustworthy". You can disagree, point out things wrong with them, even scumread us for them is there's valid reasoning, but ignoring them because we're competent? That's not what should be happening.

Hope that helped. :)
